The pie chart shows the information about the masses of fat and protein.

\newline

    

(h) 60\% of the mass of the cereal is carbohydrate.
\newline
 \\
    Complete the pie chart.
\newline

(i) Work out the mass of protein in 30~g of the cereal.
\newline

tex:

(i) (d) $4^5$

    

(e) $6^0$

    

(f) $\sqrt{16 \times 49}$

\newline
\[ 6x + 5 = 29 \]

\[ \frac{2.7 \times 42.4}{8.6 - 4.3} \]
You must show all your working.

 \\

\newline
 \\

\newline
 \\

\newline
 \\
Work out the next time both boats leave the port together.
\newline

tex:
**[2024 Oct/Nov, 0580/33, Question 3]**

(a) A wedding invitation is in the shape of a rectangle.
\newline
 \\
Draw the lines of symmetry on this rectangle.
\newline

(b) There are 98 adults and 56 children at the wedding.
\newline
 \\
Find the fraction of people who are children.
\newline
 \\
Give your fraction in its simplest form.
\newline

(c) The wedding meal starts at 13 15 and lasts for 2 hours 50 minutes.
\newline
 \\
Find the time the meal ends.
\newline

(d) The probability that it will rain at the wedding is 0.12.
\newline
 \\
Find the probability that it will not rain.
\newline

(e) (i) (f) These are the ages of the staff at the wedding.
\newline
    \[ 16   24   39   28   17   48   31   33   17   29   40   25 \]
    Complete the stem-and-leaf diagram.
\newline

    

(g) Find the range.
\newline

A wedding invitation is in the shape of a rectangle.
\newline
 \\

\newline

\newline
    \[ 16 \quad 24 \quad 39 \quad 28 \quad 17 \quad 48 \quad 31 \quad 33 \quad 17 \quad 29 \quad 40 \quad 25 \]
    Complete the stem-and-leaf diagram.
\newline

Find the range.
\newline

qtype: substitution, factorisation, forming expressions, simultaneous equations
tex:
**[2024 Oct/Nov, 0580/33, Question 4]**

(a) $n = 15r + 20c$ \\
Find the value of $r$ when $n = 180$ and $c = 3$.
\newline

(b) Factorise completely.
\newline
\[ 20p^2q - 5p \]

(c) Apples cost 35 cents each and bananas cost 14 cents each.
\newline
 \\
Write down an expression for the total cost, in cents, of $x$ apples and $y$ bananas.
\newline

(d) Solve the simultaneous equations.
\newline
 \\
You must show all your working.
\newline
\begin{align*}
    8x + 3y &= 59 \\
    5x + 7y &= 83
\end{align*}

content.content:
$n = 15r + 20c$ \\
Find the value of $r$ when $n = 180$ and $c = 3$.
\newline

Factorise completely.
\newline
\[ 20p^2q - 5p \]

Apples cost 35 cents each and bananas cost 14 cents each.
\newline
 \\
Write down an expression for the total cost, in cents, of $x$ apples and $y$ bananas.
\newline

Solve the simultaneous equations.
\newline
 \\
You must show all your working.
\newline
\begin{align*}
    8x + 3y &= 59 \\
    5x + 7y &= 83
\end{align*}

tex:
**[2024 Oct/Nov, 0580/33, Question 5]**

(a) Kurt owns some toy cars of different colours.
\newline
 \\
The pictogram shows the number of each colour.
\newline

(i) (b) Write down the colour of toy car that Kurt owns most of.
\newline

    

(c) Find the number of yellow toy cars that Kurt owns.
\newline

    

(d) Find how many more red toy cars than white toy cars Kurt owns.
\newline

    

(e) Leo buys 75 cars.
\newline
 \\
The cost of each car is 1.48 euros.
\newline
 \\
The exchange rate is 1 euro = \$1.07.
\newline
 \\
Work out the total cost of the 75 cars in dollars.
\newline

(f) Leo owns some toy vehicles.
\newline
 \\
He owns 57 cars, 12 bicycles and 42 trucks.
\newline
 \\
Find the ratio of the number of $cars : bicycles : trucks$ in its simplest form.
\newline

(g) Leo keeps his toy vehicles on a shelf.
\newline
(i) (h) The shelf is in the shape of a right-angled triangle.
\newline

    The area of the shelf is 0.3~m$^2$.
\newline
 \\
    Calculate the value of $y$.
\newline

    

(i) The shelf is 1.35 metres from the floor.
\newline
 \\
    Change 1.35 metres into centimetres.
\newline

tex:
**[2024 Oct/Nov, 0580/33, Question 6]**

(a) The diagram shows a plan of Zak's garden.
\newline

Find the perimeter of the garden.
\newline

(b) Zak records the temperature in his garden each night for one week.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Sunday & Monday & Tuesday & Wednesday & Thursday & Friday & Saturday \\ \hline
$4^\circ\text{C}$ & $1^\circ\text{C}$ & $-2^\circ\text{C}$ & $2^\circ\text{C}$ & $-5^\circ\text{C}$ & $3^\circ\text{C}$ & $-4^\circ\text{C}$ \\ \hline
\end{tabular}
(i) (c) Which night was coldest?

    

(d) Find the difference in temperature between Friday night and Saturday night.
\newline

    

(e) Zak buys pea plants, bean plants and sunflower plants in the ratio \\
$\text{peas} : \text{beans} : \text{sunflowers} = 7 : 5 : 2$.
\newline
 \\
He buys 45 bean plants.
\newline
 \\
Show that the total number of plants he buys is 126.
\newline

(f) Zak has a water barrel.
\newline
 \\
On Monday the barrel contains 120 litres of water.
\newline
 \\
On Friday the barrel contains 43.2 litres of water.
\newline
 \\
Calculate the percentage decrease of the water in the barrel.
\newline

(g) Zak drives to a shop.
\newline
 \\
The journey takes $1\frac{1}{4}$ hours.
\newline
 \\
He drives at an average speed of 57~km/h.
\newline
 \\
Calculate the distance he drives.
\newline

(h) Zak pays \$69.80 to hire a machine.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|l|c|}
\hline
\multicolumn{2}{|c|}{Machine hire charges} \\ \hline
1st day               & \$22.60             \\ \hline
Each additional day   & \$11.80             \\ \hline
\end{tabular}
Calculate the number of days he hires it for.
\newline

\newline

\newline

Zak records the temperature in his garden each night for one week.
\newline
Which night was coldest?

Find the difference in temperature between Friday night and Saturday night.
\newline

Zak buys pea plants, bean plants and sunflower plants in the ratio \\
$\text{peas} : \text{beans} : \text{sunflowers} = 7 : 5 : 2$.
\newline
 \\
He buys 45 bean plants.
\newline
 \\
Show that the total number of plants he buys is 126.
\newline

Zak has a water barrel.
\newline
 \\
On Monday the barrel contains 120 litres of water.
\newline
 \\
On Friday the barrel contains 43.2 litres of water.
\newline
 \\
Calculate the percentage decrease of the water in the barrel.
\newline

Zak drives to a shop.
\newline
 \\
The journey takes $1\frac{1}{4}$ hours.
\newline
 \\
He drives at an average speed of 57~km/h.
\newline
 \\
Calculate the distance he drives.
\newline

Zak pays \$69.80 to hire a machine.
\newline

\newline

tex:

(a) (i) (b) Complete the table of values for $y = 4x - x^2$.
\newline

    \begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
    \hline
    $x$ & $-1$ & 0 & 1 & 2 & 3 & 4 & 5 & 6 \\ \hline
    $y$ & $-5$ & 0 & 3 & 4 & 3 &   & $-5$ & \\ \hline
    \end{tabular}

(c) On the grid, draw the graph of $y = 4x - x^2$ for $-1 \le x \le 6$.
\newline

    

(d) Use your graph to solve the equation $4x - x^2 = 1$.
\newline

    

(e) The line $L$ is shown on the grid.
\newline

Find the equation of line $L$.
\newline
